basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Rūm — “The Romans” — opens on a singular prophecy: the Romans, defeated in the nearest land, will prevail “within a few years,” and on that Day the believers will rejoice in God's help. From this historical event the sūra at once rises to the cosmic signs: the creation of the heavens and the earth in truth and for an appointed term, the alternation of evening and morning, the living drawn from the dead, the lightning of fear and hope, the rain that revives the dead earth, the heaven and the earth standing fast by His command alone. At the heart of the sūra shine two verses that Qushayrī meditates with fervor: “the primordial nature (fiṭra) upon which God originated mankind” — where he sees the first stripping-bare, prior to any act or gain, where one is nothing save by God; and “He created for you spouses that you may find repose (sukūn) in them” — of which he says that the repose of bodies belongs to bodies, but the emancipated secret finds repose only in its Lord. The sūra denounces the corruption that has appeared on land and sea by the work of human hands, and is sealed on the command of patience: “Be patient, the promise of God is true.”
themes: the prophecy of the Romans' victory
the repose of the soul in union
corruption by the hands of men

versets.ar: الم
versets.tr: alif lām mīm
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the three letters as a secret of covenant. The alif points to the one who has joined with Us: he has known Our greatness; he has embraced Our trial who has known Our majesty. The lām points to the one who clings to Our door because he has tasted Our love, and remains upon Our carpet because he has witnessed Our beauty. The mīm points to the one whom Our nearness has established because he stood in Our service, and who dies in Our fidelity because he has been verified in Our friendship. The isolated letters thus open the sūra on the mystery of the inner pact.

mots.def: three opening letters, secret of covenant per Qushayrī.

versets.ar: غُلِبَتِ الرُّومُ
versets.tr: ghulibati l-rūmu
versets.trad: The Romans have been defeated
versets.commentaire: The Muslims were grieved by the Persians' victory over the Romans, then rejoiced at the announcement that the Romans would prevail. Qushayrī notes that, although disbelief gathered them all, the Romans were distinguished by faith in some of the prophets; so God was grateful to them and sent down this verse concerning them. And he marvels: what then of the one whose joy is for the religion of God, and whose grief and care are for the religion of God?

versets.ar: فِي أَدْنَى الْأَرْضِ وَهُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ غَلَبِهِمْ سَيَغْلِبُونَ
versets.tr: fī adnā l-arḍi wa-hum min baʿdi ghalabihim sa-yaghlibūna
versets.trad: in the nearest land; yet after their defeat they shall be the victors
versets.commentaire: “In the nearest land”: the defeat occurred at the margins, but the reversal is foretold. Qushayrī inserts here his meditation on the sovereignty of the decree: all command, first and last, belongs to God; victory and defeat are but the letters of one and the same divine writing, and no setback escapes the wisdom that precedes and follows it.

mots.racine: د ن و / أ ر ض

versets.ar: فِي بِضْعِ سِنِينَ لِلَّهِ الْأَمْرُ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَمِنْ بَعْدُ وَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَفْرَحُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: fī biḍʿi sinīna li-llāhi l-amru min qablu wa-min baʿdu wa-yawmaʾidhin yafraḥu l-muʾminūna
versets.trad: within a few years. To God belongs the command, before and after. And on that Day the believers shall rejoice
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes: “before,” taken absolutely, embraces pre-eternity; “after” indicates post-eternity. The meaning: the pre-eternal command is God's, the post-eternal command is God's, for the Lord of the before and the Master of the after is He. Then he sings: today is the rumor of joy; only on the Day of Meeting will it be joy in its truth. Today grief, tomorrow gladness; today tears, tomorrow encounter.

mots.racine: ب ض ع / س ن و
ف ر ح / أ م ن

versets.ar: بِنَصْرِ اللَّهِ يَنْصُرُ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: bi-naṣri llāhi yanṣuru man yashāʾu wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-raḥīmu
versets.trad: in the help of God. He grants victory to whom He wills; He is the Mighty, the Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Help comes from God alone, granted to whom He wills. Qushayrī ties this outward victory to the victory of the heart: today is the dawn of separation, tomorrow the daybreak of encounter. He is the Mighty (al-ʿAzīz) who cannot be coerced, and the Compassionate (al-Raḥīm) who, out of mercy, draws near those He has tried.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَعْدَ اللَّهِ لَا يُخْلِفُ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: waʿda llāhi lā yukhlifu llāhu waʿdahu wa-lākinna akthara l-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: It is the promise of God: God does not break His promise, but most people do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Generous One does not break His promise, all the more since truthfulness is His attribute. And he places upon the believers' lips this delicate counterpoint: from us, on the Day of the Covenant, came the promise to obey; from Him, on that Day, comes the promise of the Garden; and should some shortfall befall our promise, no defect befalls His.

mots.racine: ك ث ر / ن و س

versets.ar: يَعْلَمُونَ ظَاهِرًا مِنَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ عَنِ الْآخِرَةِ هُمْ غَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: yaʿlamūna ẓāhiran mina l-ḥayāti l-dunyā wa-hum ʿani l-ākhirati hum ghāfilūna
versets.trad: They know an outer aspect of the life of this world, but of the Hereafter they are heedless.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their absorption in the business of this lower world, and the attachment of their hearts to it, have barred them from knowing the Hereafter; yet the worth of each person lies in his knowledge of God. He cites ʿAlī: the people of this world are heedless of the Hereafter; and even those occupied with the science of the Hereafter, by their very existence, remain heedless of God.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / د ن و

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِنَ النَّاسِ بِلِقَاءِ رَبِّهِمْ لَكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yatafakkarū fī anfusihim mā khalaqa llāhu l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-mā baynahumā illā bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-ajalin musamman wa-inna kathīran mina l-nāsi bi-liqāʾi rabbihim la-kāfirūna
versets.trad: Have they not reflected within themselves? God created the heavens and the earth and what is between them only in truth and for an appointed term. Yet many people deny the Meeting with their Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever looks with a true gaze and sets the gaze in its rightful place reaps a necessary knowledge; when by the light of certainty he discerns the status of hidden things and knows the truthful promise of what is to come, he is delivered from the fatigue of wavering and conjecture. So the duty of one whose intellect has awakened is not to incline toward heedlessness in that which perfects his repose (sukūn) in God.

mots.racine: أ ج ل / س م و
ل ق ي / ر ب ب

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ كَانُوا أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً وَأَثَارُوا الْأَرْضَ وَعَمَرُوهَا أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا عَمَرُوهَا وَجَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ وَلَكِنْ كَانُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yasīrū fī l-arḍi fa-yanẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu lladhīna min qablihim kānū ashadda minhum quwwatan wa-athārū l-arḍa wa-ʿamarūhā akthara mimmā ʿamarūhā wa-jāʾathum rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināti fa-mā kāna llāhu li-yaẓlimahum wa-lākin kānū anfusahum yaẓlimūna
versets.trad: Have they not traveled the earth and seen what was the end of those before them? They were greater than them in strength; they tilled the earth and built upon it more than these have built. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs. It was not God who wronged them, but they wronged themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds four journeys here. The journey of souls through the regions of the earth to perform the acts of worship; the journey of hearts through the roaming of thought across all creatures, whose end is to seize the realities of the sciences that cool the breast; the journey of spirits in the arenas of the Unseen, rending the veils of the Kingdom, whose terminus is arrival at the place of contemplation; and the journey of secrets, ascending above all that is created, verified first in the Attributes, then wholly extinguished to all that is not the Real.

mots.racine: س ي ر / أ ر ض

mots.def: athāra: to turn over the soil.
ẓalama: to be unjust, to wrong.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ كَانَ عَاقِبَةَ الَّذِينَ أَسَاءُوا السُّوأَى أَنْ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَكَانُوا بِهَا يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: thumma kāna ʿāqibata lladhīna asāʾū l-sūʾā an kadhdhabū bi-āyāti llāhi wa-kānū bihā yastahziʾūna
versets.trad: Then the end of those who did evil was the worst, for they denied the signs of God and mocked them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the law of recompense into three limpid images: whoever sows thorns does not reap the rose; whoever cultivates weeds does not pluck fruit; whoever takes the road of error does not alight in the courtyard of right guidance. The grievous end is but the ripe fruit of the seed of denial.

mots.def: al-sūʾā: the extreme evil, the worst end.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ يَبْدَأُ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu yabdaʾu l-khalqa thumma yuʿīduhu thumma ilayhi turjaʿūna
versets.trad: God originates the creation, then renews it, then to Him you shall be returned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers the arc of existence into a formula of pure sovereignty: He begins the creation as He wills, then brings it back when He wills and as He wills. Nothing precedes His will, nothing compels His return: origination and recall rest in His freedom alone.

mots.racine: ب د أ / خ ل ق

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يُبْلِسُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma taqūmu l-sāʿatu yublisu l-mujrimūna
versets.trad: And on the Day the Hour arrives, the guilty shall despair.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes what crushes the criminal on that Day: the sight, in full evidence, of what he denied in this world; then, added to that, the despair that follows certainty — this is what rends their livers and consummates their trial. The torment is not only outward: it is the irruption of a knowledge come too late.

mots.racine: ق و م / س و ع

mots.def: ablasa: to be struck dumb with despair.

versets.ar: وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُمْ مِنْ شُرَكَائِهِمْ شُفَعَاءُ وَكَانُوا بِشُرَكَائِهِمْ كَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lam yakun lahum min shurakāʾihim shufaʿāʾu wa-kānū bi-shurakāʾihim kāfirīna
versets.trad: They shall have no intercessors among those they associated with God, and they will deny those they associated with Him.
versets.commentaire: On the Day of the Hour, the idols they had set up turn against them. Qushayrī sums up the reversal in a terse phrase: then the enmity of some prevails over others. What they worshipped becomes their accuser, and the false alliance topples into mutual hatred.

mots.def: sharīk: associate set up with God.

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَفَرَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma taqūmu l-sāʿatu yawmaʾidhin yatafarraqūna
versets.trad: And on the Day the Hour arrives, on that Day they shall part from one another.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns this verse into a litany of parting. One group will be of union, one of separation; one group for the Garden and grace, one for the punishment and trial; one in bliss, one in the blaze; one in reward, one in torment; one in farewell, one in encounter.

versets.ar: فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ فَهُمْ فِي رَوْضَةٍ يُحْبَرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ammā lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū l-ṣāliḥāti fa-hum fī rawḍatin yuḥbarūna
versets.trad: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, they shall be made joyful in a meadow.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the believers' beatitude into a flowering image: here they are in meadows and groves. Faith joined to righteous deeds finds its term in a garden of joy, where the verdant soul gathers the fruit of its fidelity.

mots.racine: ر و ض / ح ب ر
mots.sens: a meadow where they are made joyful
mots.def: rawḍa: meadow; ḥubira: to be gladdened.

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَلِقَاءِ الْآخِرَةِ فَأُولَئِكَ فِي الْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ammā lladhīna kafarū wa-kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-liqāʾi l-ākhirati fa-ulāʾika fī l-ʿadhābi muḥḍarūna
versets.trad: But as for those who disbelieved and denied Our signs and the Meeting of the Hereafter, those shall be brought into the punishment.
versets.commentaire: In exact counterpoint to the preceding verse, Qushayrī cuts it short with a single word: here they are in perdition and ruin. Those who denied the signs and the Meeting have only the forced presence at the place of torment — the reverse of the garden promised to the believers.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / أ ي ي

versets.ar: فَسُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ حِينَ تُمْسُونَ وَحِينَ تُصْبِحُونَ
versets.tr: fa-subḥāna llāhi ḥīna tumsūna wa-ḥīna tuṣbiḥūna
versets.trad: So glorify God when you reach the evening and when you reach the morning!
themes: glorification at the times of day
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he whose morning is for God, his day is blessed thereby; he whose evening is by God, his night is blessed thereby. And he distinguishes two ranks of servant: the one whose morning opens with his worship and whose evening closes with his obedience — and the one whose morning opens with His contemplation and whose dusk opens with the honor of His nearness.

versets.ar: وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَعَشِيًّا وَحِينَ تُظْهِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lahu l-ḥamdu fī l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-ʿashiyyan wa-ḥīna tuẓhirūna
versets.trad: And to Him be praise, in the heavens and on the earth, at nightfall and when you reach midday!
versets.commentaire: According to the exegetes, these two verses contain the five canonical prayers. Qushayrī sees in them above all the Real's will that His intimates renew the covenant five times by day and night: you then stand upon the carpet of intimate colloquy (munājāt), making up, in the interval between two prayers, for the sudden stumblings of shortcomings.

versets.ar: يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِّ وَيُحْيِي الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا وَكَذَلِكَ تُخْرَجُونَ
versets.tr: yukhriju l-ḥayya mina l-mayyiti wa-yukhriju l-mayyita mina l-ḥayyi wa-yuḥyī l-arḍa baʿda mawtihā wa-kadhālika tukhrajūna
versets.trad: He brings forth the living from the dead and brings forth the dead from the living, and He revives the earth after its death. Thus shall you be brought forth.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the verse to the heart: He brings forth the living from the dead — the bird from the egg, the animal from the drop; and the dead from the living — the egg from the bird; and the believer from the disbeliever, and the disbeliever from the believer. He brings forth certain states amid others: contraction (qabḍ) amid the times of expansion (basṭ), and expansion amid the times of contraction. As He revives the earth by rain, so does He resurrect the hearts.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ح ي ي
mots.def: akhraja: to bring forth; ḥayy: living.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / أ ر ض

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ أَنْ خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ إِذَا أَنْتُمْ بَشَرٌ تَنْتَشِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi an khalaqakum min turābin thumma idhā antum basharun tantashirūna
versets.trad: And among His signs is that He created you from dust; then behold, you are human beings spreading abroad.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He created Adam from earth, then from Adam the progeny; thus He reminds them of their lineage, lest they grow proud of their states. And he overturns the perspective: the origin is clay, but what counts is the nurturing (tarbiya), not the clay (turba); worth belongs to the One from whom the thing comes, not to created essences.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ت ر ب

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَاجًا لِتَسْكُنُوا إِلَيْهَا وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ مَوَدَّةً وَرَحْمَةً إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi an khalaqa lakum min anfusikum azwājan li-taskunū ilayhā wa-jaʿala baynakum mawaddatan wa-raḥmatan inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yatafakkarūna
versets.trad: And among His signs is that He created for you, from yourselves, spouses that you may find repose in them, and He placed between you affection and mercy. Surely in that are signs for a people who reflect.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He brought the like to the like, bound form to form, and made some find repose (sukūn) in others; but this holds for bodies and outward forms. As for the spirits, their company with bodies is constraint and not willingness; and as for the secrets, they are emancipated: they do not dwell in ruins nor are they defiled by any attachment. Perfect sukūn is only in God, whose affection and mercy are the true bond.

mots.sens: that you may find repose in them

mots.racine: و د د / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ خَلْقُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاخْتِلَافُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَأَلْوَانِكُمْ إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِلْعَالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi khalqu l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-khtilāfu alsinatikum wa-alwānikum inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-l-ʿālimīna
versets.trad: And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your tongues and your colors. Surely in that are signs for those who know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets the heaven in its loftiness against the earth in its lowliness, the one with its stars, the other with its regions. Then he marvels: among His signs is the diversity of the languages of the earth's dwellers and the diversity of the glorifications of the angels who people the heaven; and the fact that each thing is distinguished by its own law is a just witness and a truthful proof that they all confide to the thoughts of the awakened and proclaim of themselves that they belong to the decree of the Mighty, the All-Knowing.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / س م و
خ ل ف / ل س ن

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ مَنَامُكُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ وَابْتِغَاؤُكُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi manāmukum bi-l-layli wa-l-nahāri wa-btighāʾukum min faḍlihi inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yasmaʿūna
versets.trad: And among His signs is your sleep by night and day and your seeking of His bounty. Surely in that are signs for a people who hear.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of sleep a parable of the grave. Sleep overcomes you without your choice, and you awaken with no power of your own: a proof of your death and of your resurrection at the time of your waking. And in sleep you see what gladdens you and what harms you, in a thousand diverse states — so too the dead in his grave: God alone knows his state, what he meets of his good and his evil, his benefit and his harm.

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ يُرِيكُمُ الْبَرْقَ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَيُحْيِي بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi yurīkumu l-barqa khawfan wa-ṭamaʿan wa-yunazzilu mina l-samāʾi māʾan fa-yuḥyī bihi l-arḍa baʿda mawtihā inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: And among His signs is that He shows you the lightning, a cause of fear and hope, and sends down water from the sky by which He revives the earth after its death. Surely in that are signs for a people who reason.
versets.commentaire: The lightning casts into hearts hope and expectation. Qushayrī makes it the image of fleeting spiritual states: the subtle states are like flashes of lightning; they have been named gleams (lawāʾiḥ), then flashes (lawāmiʿ), then risings (ṭawāliʿ), then dawns (shawāriq). The gleams belong to the beginnings of the sciences; the flashes pertain to the intelligences; the risings, to the gnoses; the dawns, to unification (tawḥīd).

mots.racine: خ و ف / ط م ع
ن ز ل / م و ه

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ أَنْ تَقُومَ السَّمَاءُ وَالْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِ ثُمَّ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ دَعْوَةً مِنَ الْأَرْضِ إِذَا أَنْتُمْ تَخْرُجُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi an taqūma l-samāʾu wa-l-arḍu bi-amrihi thumma idhā daʿākum daʿwatan mina l-arḍi idhā antum takhrujūna
versets.trad: And among His signs is that the heaven and the earth stand fast by His command. Then, when He calls you with a single call from the earth, behold, you will come forth.
themes: heaven and earth by His command
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in the standing of heaven and earth the announcement of a great upheaval: He will annihilate these cycles, transform these conditions, substitute other states for the present ones: death then life, return and before it origination, grave then resurrection, reproach in the grave then reckoning after resurrection. The whole cosmic order hangs suspended on the single command (amr).

mots.racine: ق و م / س م و

mots.sens: He calls you with a call

versets.ar: وَلَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ كُلٌّ لَهُ قَانِتُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lahu man fī l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi kullun lahu qānitūna
versets.trad: To Him belong all who are in the heavens and on the earth; all are devoutly obedient to Him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers into four prepositions the relation of all being to God: all this is His by possession; and from Him these things proceed by origination, by Him they exist, and to Him they return. Universal obedience (qunūt) is but the trace of this total belonging.

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَبْدَأُ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ وَهُوَ أَهْوَنُ عَلَيْهِ وَلَهُ الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلَى فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī yabdaʾu l-khalqa thumma yuʿīduhu wa-huwa ahwanu ʿalayhi wa-lahu l-mathalu l-aʿlā fī l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: It is He who originates the creation, then renews it, and that is easier for Him. To Him belongs the highest attribute in the heavens and the earth. He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī clarifies: “that is easier for Him” — according to your estimation and measure; for in truth ease and difficulty cannot be predicated of the Real. And he unfolds “the highest attribute”: His is the supreme attribute in existence by the right of priority, in generosity by nobility, in power by universality, in knowledge by the encompassing of all, in majesty by the splendor of glory and beauty.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ع ل و
ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: ضَرَبَ لَكُمْ مَثَلًا مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ هَلْ لَكُمْ مِنْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ مِنْ شُرَكَاءَ فِي مَا رَزَقْنَاكُمْ فَأَنْتُمْ فِيهِ سَوَاءٌ تَخَافُونَهُمْ كَخِيفَتِكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ كَذَلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: ḍaraba lakum mathalan min anfusikum hal lakum min mā malakat aymānukum min shurakāʾa fī mā razaqnākum fa-antum fīhi sawāʾun takhāfūnahum ka-khīfatikum anfusakum kadhālika nufaṣṣilu l-āyāti li-qawmin yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: He sets forth for you a parable from yourselves: do you have, among those your right hands possess, partners in what We have provided you, so that you are equal in it, fearing them as you fear one another? Thus do We expound the signs for a people who reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī develops the argument a fortiori: if you would not accept equality between yourselves and your slaves, though you are of the same nature in every respect — save that the Law has made you their owners — what then of the One who has never ceased to be and will never cease? Can it be conceived that His servants are His equals, that His owned should be His partner? Exalted is God far above that!

mots.racine: ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.def: ḍaraba mathalan: to give an example.

mots.racine: م ل ك / ي م ن

versets.ar: بَلِ اتَّبَعَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا أَهْوَاءَهُمْ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ فَمَنْ يَهْدِي مَنْ أَضَلَّ اللَّهُ وَمَا لَهُمْ مِنْ نَاصِرِينَ
versets.tr: bali ttabaʿa lladhīna ẓalamū ahwāʾahum bi-ghayri ʿilmin fa-man yahdī man aḍalla llāhu wa-mā lahum min nāṣirīna
versets.trad: Rather, the wrongdoers follow their desires without knowledge. Who then can guide those whom God has led astray? They shall have no helpers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the worst of injustices is to follow desire, for it is the neighbor of associationism — and he cites: “Have you seen the one who took his desire for his god?” Whoever follows his craving contradicts the good pleasure of his Master; by setting the thing out of its place, he becomes a wrongdoer, like the disobedient who sets transgression in the place of obedience; so this one, following his desire instead of his Master's good pleasure, persists in injustice.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ه و ي
mots.def: ittabaʿa: to follow; hawā: craving, passion.

versets.ar: فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًا فِطْرَتَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِخَلْقِ اللَّهِ ذَلِكَ الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-aqim wajhaka li-l-dīni ḥanīfan fiṭrata llāhi llatī faṭara l-nāsa ʿalayhā lā tabdīla li-khalqi llāhi dhālika l-dīnu l-qayyimu wa-lākinna akthara l-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: So set your face to the religion as a pure monotheist, the primordial nature (fiṭra) of God upon which He originated mankind. There is no altering the creation of God. That is the upright religion, but most people do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: purify your intention toward God, keep your covenant with God, devote your work — in your stillnesses, your movements, and all your acts — to God alone. On the fiṭra he meditates: hold fast to the primordial nature upon which God fashioned mankind — that is, He established them upon it before any act or gain, any associationism or disbelief, proceeded from them; just as they had neither faith nor goodness, they had neither ingratitude nor disobedience. Such is the upright religion: to return to the primal stripping-bare where one is nothing save by God.

mots.racine: ق و م / و ج ه
mots.def: aqāma: to set upright; wajh: face, direction.

mots.racine: د ي ن / ق و م

versets.ar: مُنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ وَاتَّقُوهُ وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَلَا تَكُونُوا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: munībīna ilayhi wa-ttaqūhu wa-aqīmū l-ṣalāta wa-lā takūnū mina l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: Turning to Him in repentance; fear Him, establish the prayer, and be not among the associators,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives repentance (ināba) its fullness: returning to God entirely, with no remnant left; qualified by His accord, turned away in every respect from His discord; guarded from the small sin as from the great, from the little as from the much; preferring His good pleasure, whether easy or hard; establishing the prayer in its pillars, rites, and proprieties outwardly, verified in the keeping of its virtues inwardly.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
mots.def: aqāma l-ṣalāt: to observe the prayer.
mushrik: one who associates with God.

versets.ar: مِنَ الَّذِينَ فَرَّقُوا دِينَهُمْ وَكَانُوا شِيَعًا كُلُّ حِزْبٍ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ فَرِحُونَ
versets.tr: mina lladhīna farraqū dīnahum wa-kānū shiyaʿan kullu ḥizbin bi-mā ladayhim fariḥūna
versets.trad: of those who split up their religion and became sects, each faction rejoicing in what it holds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the illusion of the factions: they planted in their world under the veil of heedlessness, the obstinacy of ignorance and torpor; they trusted their conjectures, took up dwelling in the vehicle of their fancies, enriched themselves from another's purse, and supposed they held something. But when the mist of their moment dissipates and the cloud of their denial is torn away, their joy turns to grief: they gain the certainty that they were in error and halted only at the homelands of ignorance.

mots.racine: ف ر ق / د ي ن

versets.ar: وَإِذَا مَسَّ النَّاسَ ضُرٌّ دَعَوْا رَبَّهُمْ مُنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ إِذَا أَذَاقَهُمْ مِنْهُ رَحْمَةً إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِنْهُمْ بِرَبِّهِمْ يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā massa l-nāsa ḍurrun daʿaw rabbahum munībīna ilayhi thumma idhā adhāqahum minhu raḥmatan idhā farīqun minhum bi-rabbihim yushrikūna
versets.trad: When harm touches people, they call upon their Lord, turning to Him in repentance. Then, when He makes them taste a mercy from Him, behold, a party of them associate others with their Lord,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the trial overshadows them, when temptation strikes, when affliction touches them, they return to God wholly, imploring His aid, seeking refuge in His gentleness, beseeching Him to lift their distress. But when He bestows by removing what struck them and regards them with kindness — behold, a party of them, not all, returns to its blameworthy habits of ingratitude, repaying His favor with forgetfulness: these have neither covenant nor faithfulness, and no purity in their affection.

mots.racine: م س س / ض ر ر
ذ و ق / ر ح م
mots.sens: He makes them taste a mercy

versets.ar: لِيَكْفُرُوا بِمَا آتَيْنَاهُمْ فَتَمَتَّعُوا فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: li-yakfurū bi-mā ātaynāhum fa-tamattaʿū fa-sawfa taʿlamūna
versets.trad: denying what We have granted them. So enjoy yourselves; soon you shall know!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī inscribes here the cyclical law of trial and forgetting: soon there will befall them again what struck them before; then they return to supplication and resume their humility; but when He heals and calms them, they go back to the source of their faults. The threat “you shall know” hovers over this inconstancy.

mots.def: kafara: to be ungrateful, to deny.
tamattaʿa: to enjoy a passing pleasure.

versets.ar: أَمْ أَنْزَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانًا فَهُوَ يَتَكَلَّمُ بِمَا كَانُوا بِهِ يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: am anzalnā ʿalayhim sulṭānan fa-huwa yatakallamu bi-mā kānū bihi yushrikūna
versets.trad: Or have We sent down upon them an authority that speaks of what they associate with Him?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī denounces the groundlessness of associationism: He has shown that they built their way upon no principle, that they followed their cravings in what they invented, and that they founded their doctrines without any law from God, without argument or manifest proof. No heavenly “authority” sanctions what they associate with Him.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا أَذَقْنَا النَّاسَ رَحْمَةً فَرِحُوا بِهَا وَإِنْ تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ إِذَا هُمْ يَقْنَطُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā adhaqnā l-nāsa raḥmatan fariḥū bihā wa-in tuṣibhum sayyiʾatun bi-mā qaddamat aydīhim idhā hum yaqnaṭūna
versets.trad: When We make people taste a mercy, they rejoice in it; but if an evil befalls them for what their hands have sent forth, behold, they despair.
themes: the joy and despair of souls
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts ordinary souls with the great: the accidents of their states toss them about: if it is a blessing, toward joy; if a trial, toward despair and grief. But such is not the trait of the highest, and he cites the verse: “so that you grieve not over what escapes you, nor rejoice over what is given you.” The heart rooted in God neither sways with joy nor lists with distress.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ر ح م

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw anna llāha yabsuṭu l-rizqa li-man yashāʾu wa-yaqdiru inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Have they not seen that God extends provision abundantly to whom He wills and measures it? Surely in that are signs for a people who believe.
themes: the expansion and measure of provision
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the twofold hand of provision a lesson of exclusive attachment: the servant should suspend his heart upon none but God, for what grieves him is lifted only by God, and what gladdens him is obtained only from God. The expansion (basṭ) that gladdens and consoles him comes from Him; the contraction (qabḍ) that afflicts and frightens him comes from Him. The duty is therefore to remain at the threshold of the secrets and to cut off thoughts from all that is not He.

mots.racine: ب س ط / ر ز ق
mots.def: basaṭa: to spread out; rizq: provision.

versets.ar: فَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبَى حَقَّهُ وَالْمِسْكِينَ وَابْنَ السَّبِيلِ ذَلِكَ خَيْرٌ لِلَّذِينَ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ اللَّهِ وَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: fa-āti dhā l-qurbā ḥaqqahu wa-l-miskīna wa-bna l-sabīli dhālika khayrun li-lladhīna yurīdūna wajha llāhi wa-ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūna
versets.trad: So give the near relative his due, and the poor, and the wayfarer. That is best for those who desire the Face of God; and those are the ones who prosper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two kinships: that of blood and that of religion; and the kinship of religion is more pressing, more worthy of mutual aid. Then he defines the aspirant (murīd): the one who prefers the right of God to the share of his soul; his preference for the Face of God is more complete than the care of his own state, and his ardor to relieve the kin and the poor takes precedence over the regard he gives to himself and his own.

mots.racine: ب ن و / س ب ل
mots.def: ibn al-sabīl: lit. son of the road.

versets.ar: وَمَا آتَيْتُمْ مِنْ رِبًا لِيَرْبُوَ فِي أَمْوَالِ النَّاسِ فَلَا يَرْبُو عِنْدَ اللَّهِ وَمَا آتَيْتُمْ مِنْ زَكَاةٍ تُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ اللَّهِ فَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْمُضْعِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā ātaytum min riban li-yarbuwa fī amwāli l-nāsi fa-lā yarbū ʿinda llāhi wa-mā ātaytum min zakātin turīdūna wajha llāhi fa-ulāʾika humu l-muḍʿifūna
versets.trad: Whatever usury you give to increase within the wealth of people yields no increase with God; but whatever alms you give, desiring the Face of God — those are the ones who receive a manifold reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī purifies the meaning of alms: to give the zakāt seeking the Face of God, without enslaving the poor by the kindness with which you favor him; better still: the best alms is that given to a hostile relative, so that the gift be for God alone, stripped of any share returning to you. Those will see their reward multiplied: they have subdued their souls by thwarting them, and won the recompense from God. He recalls too the zakāt of the body, of the heart, and of the secret.

mots.def: muḍʿif: one whose recompense is doubled.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ رَزَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ هَلْ مِنْ شُرَكَائِكُمْ مَنْ يَفْعَلُ مِنْ ذَلِكُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī khalaqakum thumma razaqakum thumma yumītukum thumma yuḥyīkum hal min shurakāʾikum man yafʿalu min dhālikum min shayʾin subḥānahu wa-taʿālā ʿammā yushrikūna
versets.trad: It is God who created you, then provided for you, then causes you to die, then gives you life. Is there among your partners any that does anything of that? Glory be to Him, and exalted is He above what they associate!
themes: the deaths and lives of the heart
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the four verbs. He nourishes you with milk, then with food, then with the provisions of hearts and secrets — faith and gnosis; with the provisions of assistance — obedience and worship; with the provisions of the tongue — remembrance. Then “He causes you to die”: by the falling away of your desires, by making you die to your witnessings. Then “He gives you life”: by the life of your hearts, then by making you live through your Lord. The gift has no cause in any merit of the soul: the one made ample is so by grace, the one restricted by decree.

versets.ar: ظَهَرَ الْفَسَادُ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِي النَّاسِ لِيُذِيقَهُمْ بَعْضَ الَّذِي عَمِلُوا لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: ẓahara l-fasādu fī l-barri wa-l-baḥri bi-mā kasabat aydī l-nāsi li-yudhīqahum baʿḍa lladhī ʿamilū laʿallahum yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: Corruption has appeared on land and sea for what the hands of people have earned, that He may make them taste some of what they have done, so that they may return.
themes: the corruption of land and sea
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the verse: the land (barr) signifies the soul, and the sea (baḥr) the heart. The corruption of the land comes through devouring the unlawful and trespassing the forbidden; the corruption of the sea, through heedlessness and blameworthy traits — bad resolve, envy, rancor, the will to evil. To persist in discord is the greatest corruption of the heart, just as resolve toward good deeds before performing them is among the greatest of goods.

mots.racine: ظ ه ر / ف س د
ب ر ر / ب ح ر
ك س ب / ي د ي
mots.sens: what the hands of people have earned

versets.ar: قُلْ سِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَانْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلُ كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: qul sīrū fī l-arḍi fa-nẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu lladhīna min qablu kāna aktharuhum mushrikīna
versets.trad: Say: “Travel the earth and see what was the end of those who came before; most of them were associators.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the journey into an inward process: “Travel” by reflection, and seek the Real by the roaming of thoughts; “see” what was the state of your likes and peers who preceded you, and measure yourself against them in all your states. On “most of them were associators” he slips in: they were the most in number, but in truth the least in weight and worth.

mots.racine: ك ث ر / ش ر ك

versets.ar: فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ الْقَيِّمِ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ يَوْمٌ لَا مَرَدَّ لَهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصَّدَّعُونَ
versets.tr: fa-aqim wajhaka li-l-dīni l-qayyimi min qabli an yaʾtiya yawmun lā maradda lahu mina llāhi yawmaʾidhin yaṣṣaddaʿūna
versets.trad: So set your face to the upright religion before there comes from God a Day that none can turn back. On that Day they shall be split apart:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī adds to uprightness the necessity of spiritual schooling: purify your intention and make your resolve sincere for the upright religion, by accord and following, without arrogating the matter to yourself by way of innovation. For whoever does not take training from the one who is the tongue of his age, and does not receive the remembrance from his mouth, finds his loss more complete than his gain, and his lack more general than his profit.

mots.racine: د ي ن / ق و م

versets.ar: مَنْ كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُ وَمَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَلِأَنْفُسِهِمْ يَمْهَدُونَ
versets.tr: man kafara fa-ʿalayhi kufruhu wa-man ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-li-anfusihim yamhadūna
versets.trad: Whoever disbelieves, upon him is his disbelief; and whoever does righteousness, they prepare a resting-place for themselves.
versets.commentaire: The verse traces the exact recompense: disbelief falls back upon its author, the good deed profits the one who performs it. In the wake of Qushayrī, one hears that each prepares his own couch: the godly arranges for his soul a place of repose, while the denier loads upon himself alone the burden of his denial.

mots.def: mahada: to arrange, to make ready the bed.

versets.ar: لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ إِنَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: li-yajziya lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū l-ṣāliḥāti min faḍlihi innahu lā yuḥibbu l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: That He may reward, out of His bounty, those who believed and did righteous deeds. Indeed, He does not love the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: The believers' reward proceeds “from His bounty” (min faḍlihi), not from any debt. In the spirit of Qushayrī, this is to say that the recompense of the faithful belongs to pure giving and not to bargaining: God rewards by largesse what the deed, of itself, could never purchase; and His love turns away from whoever has turned away through denial.

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ أَنْ يُرْسِلَ الرِّيَاحَ مُبَشِّرَاتٍ وَلِيُذِيقَكُمْ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ وَلِتَجْرِيَ الْفُلْكُ بِأَمْرِهِ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi an yursila l-riyāḥa mubashshirātin wa-li-yudhīqakum min raḥmatihi wa-li-tajriya l-fulku bi-amrihi wa-li-tabtaghū min faḍlihi wa-laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: And among His signs is that He sends the winds as bearers of glad tidings, to make you taste of His mercy, that the ship may sail by His command, that you may seek of His bounty, and that you may be grateful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the winds the breaths of spiritual states: He sends the winds of hope upon the hearts of the servants, sweeping away the dust of fear and the foam of despair; then He pours upon them the rains of assistance, which carry them to the carpet of striving. He sends the winds of expansion (basṭ) upon the spirits of the intimates, purifying them of the desolation of contraction; and the winds of unification (tawḥīd), which blow upon the secrets of the elect and announce to them the permanence of union. But it is a repose by Him, after an uprooting from you.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ر و ح
ج ر ي / ف ل ك

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ رُسُلًا إِلَى قَوْمِهِمْ فَجَاءُوهُمْ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَانْتَقَمْنَا مِنَ الَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا وَكَانَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا نَصْرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad arsalnā min qablika rusulan ilā qawmihim fa-jāʾūhum bi-l-bayyināti fa-ntaqamnā mina lladhīna ajramū wa-kāna ḥaqqan ʿalaynā naṣru l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: And We did send before you messengers to their people, and they came to them with clear proofs; then We took vengeance on those who committed crimes. And it was incumbent upon Us to help the believers.
themes: the help due to the believers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī comments with striking vigor: whoever received the messengers with assent attained the quintessence of verification; whoever opposed them with denial tasted eternal punishment. We took vengeance on the criminals, seized them whence they did not expect, confounded what they hoped for. As for the help due to the believers: We raised them upon the necks of their enemies, ruined the homelands of their adversaries, quenched their fires — their suns stayed eclipsed, and the cunning of Our might covered them entirely.

mots.racine: ن ص ر / أ م ن

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي يُرْسِلُ الرِّيَاحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا فَيَبْسُطُهُ فِي السَّمَاءِ كَيْفَ يَشَاءُ وَيَجْعَلُهُ كِسَفًا فَتَرَى الْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَالِهِ فَإِذَا أَصَابَ بِهِ مَنْ يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī yursilu l-riyāḥa fa-tuthīru saḥāban fa-yabsuṭuhu fī l-samāʾi kayfa yashāʾu wa-yajʿaluhu kisafan fa-tarā l-wadqa yakhruju min khilālihi fa-idhā aṣāba bihi man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi idhā hum yastabshirūna
versets.trad: It is God who sends the winds, and they raise a cloud, which He spreads in the sky as He wills and breaks into fragments; then you see the rain issuing from its midst. And when He makes it fall upon whom He wills of His servants, behold, they rejoice,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the image to mystical rapture: He sends the winds of His tenderness and generosity, heralds of His nearness; then He rains the largesse of His unseen upon their secrets by His gentleness, folds away the carpet of reserve before the courtyards of His proximity, raises the pavilions of majesty at the places of His disclosure, scatters upon them the flowers of His intimacy, then manifests Himself to them by the realities of His holiness and waters them from His hand with the drink of His love; and after effacing them from their attributes, He renders them lucid — not by themselves, but by Himself: expressions there fall mute and allusions are blotted out.

mots.racine: ث و ر / س ح ب
mots.def: athāra: to stir up; saḥāb: cloud.
istabshara: to exult at good news.

versets.ar: وَإِنْ كَانُوا مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ لَمُبْلِسِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in kānū min qabli an yunazzala ʿalayhim min qablihi la-mublisīna
versets.trad: though before it was sent down upon them, before that, they were in despair.
versets.commentaire: The verse paints the reversal: just before the rain they were dumb with despair, and behold, they exult. In the line of Qushayrī, this is the very figure of divine grace: it comes at the moment when the soul, stripped of all expectation, clings only to the pure gift; despair precedes the downpour of mercy as night precedes the dawn.

mots.racine: ق ب ل / ن ز ل
mots.def: nuzzila: to be sent down from above.

versets.ar: فَانْظُرْ إِلَى آثَارِ رَحْمَتِ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُحْيِي الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا إِنَّ ذَلِكَ لَمُحْيِي الْمَوْتَى وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: fa-nẓur ilā āthāri raḥmati llāhi kayfa yuḥyī l-arḍa baʿda mawtihā inna dhālika la-muḥyī l-mawtā wa-huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: So behold the traces of God's mercy: how He revives the earth after its death. Surely that One is the Reviver of the dead, and He is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī extends the revival to all degrees of being. He revives the earth by its flowers and lights at the coming of the rains; He revives the souls after their flight and disposes them to good after their torpor; He revives the hearts after their heedlessness by the lights of the presences, and they return to the permanence of remembrance; He revives the spirits after their veiling by the lights of the contemplations, and their suns rise from the castle of felicity; He revives even the secrets, and there every trace of otherness is effaced: no dweller remains in the dwelling, no trace, for the assaults of the realities tolerate not one atom of the attributes of creatures — there is the sovereignty of God.

mots.racine: أ ث ر / ر ح م
ح ي ي / أ ر ض
ح ي ي / م و ت

versets.ar: وَلَئِنْ أَرْسَلْنَا رِيحًا فَرَأَوْهُ مُصْفَرًّا لَظَلُّوا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ يَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laʾin arsalnā rīḥan fa-raʾawhu muṣfarran la-ẓallū min baʿdihi yakfurūna
versets.trad: Yet if We send a wind and they see it turn the crops yellow, they would persist after that in disbelief.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws a law of the heart: when insight is closed to perception, the blindness lasts through all times; likewise, those upon whom wretchedness has been confirmed are dragged by it to itself — even were they to take the Garden for a dwelling. One reversal of nature suffices to reveal their deep-seated ingratitude.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ر و ح

versets.ar: فَإِنَّكَ لَا تُسْمِعُ الْمَوْتَى وَلَا تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَّ الدُّعَاءَ إِذَا وَلَّوْا مُدْبِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-innaka lā tusmiʿu l-mawtā wa-lā tusmiʿu l-ṣumma l-duʿāʾa idhā wallaw mudbirīna
versets.trad: Indeed, you cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn away in flight.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes spiritual death and deafness: whoever has lost the original life is not revived by incantations or amulets; and whoever bears within his innermost a deafness to hearing the Reality, the outward ear avails him nothing, even before the most pressing argument. One cannot make the call heard by him who turns away from it.

mots.racine: س م ع / م و ت
mots.sens: you cannot make the dead hear

mots.racine: و ل ي / د ب ر
mots.def: wallā: to turn away; mudbir: one who flees.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَنْتَ بِهَادِ الْعُمْيِ عَنْ ضَلَالَتِهِمْ إِنْ تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِآيَاتِنَا فَهُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā anta bi-hādi l-ʿumyi ʿan ḍalālatihim in tusmiʿu illā man yuʾminu bi-āyātinā fa-hum muslimūna
versets.trad: Nor can you guide the blind out of their error. You can make hear only those who believe in Our signs and who, then, are submitted (muslimūn).
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī extends the preceding lesson: just as one cannot make the call heard by the deaf, one cannot guide the blind out of their error. The word reaches only the one whose inner ear is open: only those hear who believe in the signs and surrender (islām) into the hands of God.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ع م ي
mots.def: hādī: one who guides; aʿmā: blind.
muslim: one who surrenders to God.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ ضَعْفٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ضَعْفٍ قُوَّةً ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ضَعْفًا وَشَيْبَةً يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَاءُ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيمُ الْقَدِيرُ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī khalaqakum min ḍaʿfin thumma jaʿala min baʿdi ḍaʿfin quwwatan thumma jaʿala min baʿdi quwwatin ḍaʿfan wa-shaybatan yakhluqu mā yashāʾu wa-huwa l-ʿalīmu l-qadīru
versets.trad: It is God who created you from weakness, then after weakness gave strength, then after strength gave weakness and old age. He creates what He wills, and He is the All-Knowing, the All-Powerful.
themes: the ages of weakness and strength
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the three ages onto the spiritual path: at the beginning, the weakness of the novice, in the wavering and bewilderment of the quest; then the strength of union; then the weakness of extinction (khumūd). It is also said: first the weakness of the intellect, bound to proof; then the strength of exposition at the time of gnosis, by the force of presence; then the weakness of extinction, for extinction follows presence and leaves behind no trace. He closes with the hadith: “Make me live humble, die humble, and resurrect me in the company of the humble.”

mots.racine: ع ل م / ق د ر

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يُقْسِمُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ مَا لَبِثُوا غَيْرَ سَاعَةٍ كَذَلِكَ كَانُوا يُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma taqūmu l-sāʿatu yuqsimu l-mujrimūna mā labithū ghayra sāʿatin kadhālika kānū yuʾfakūna
versets.trad: And on the Day the Hour arrives, the guilty will swear they tarried but an hour. Thus were they ever deluded...,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives two reasons for this oath of the guilty: either because they were dead, and the dead has no perception; or because they reckoned the torment of the grave as slight in comparison with what they see on that Day. Yet the people of verification will inform them of the true length of their sojourn underground; their denial is but a continuation of the blindness in which they were entangled.

mots.racine: ل ب ث / س و ع

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ وَالْإِيمَانَ لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْبَعْثِ فَهَذَا يَوْمُ الْبَعْثِ وَلَكِنَّكُمْ كُنْتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma wa-l-īmāna laqad labithtum fī kitābi llāhi ilā yawmi l-baʿthi fa-hādhā yawmu l-baʿthi wa-lākinnakum kuntum lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: But those who were given knowledge and faith will say: “You have indeed tarried, according to the decree of the Book of God, until the Day of Resurrection. So this is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know it.”
themes: the testimony of those given knowledge
versets.commentaire: To the illusion of the guilty answers the word of the people of knowledge and faith. In the wake of Qushayrī, their testimony restores the true measure: those whom knowledge has enlightened know the real length of the sojourn in the grave, where the criminals deemed it brief. Knowledge joined to faith sees time as it is inscribed in the Book of God, and recognizes the Day when it comes.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ل م
mots.def: ūtiya: to be given; ʿilm: knowledge.

versets.ar: فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لَا يَنْفَعُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ
versets.tr: fa-yawmaʾidhin lā yanfaʿu lladhīna ẓalamū maʿdhiratuhum wa-lā hum yustaʿtabūna
versets.trad: So on that Day the excuse of the wrongdoers will not avail them, nor will they be allowed to make amends.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals the lot of the unjust: what they utter belongs to that denial they displayed at the whim of their ignorance; so their excuse is not heard and their harm is not averted. The Day of truth leaves no room for pleading: the hour for returning to grace has passed.

mots.sens: they are not asked to make amends
mots.def: ustuʿtiba: to be invited to make redress.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِي هَذَا الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ وَلَئِنْ جِئْتَهُمْ بِآيَةٍ لَيَقُولَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا مُبْطِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ḍarabnā li-l-nāsi fī hādhā l-qurʾāni min kulli mathalin wa-laʾin jiʾtahum bi-āyatin la-yaqūlanna lladhīna kafarū in antum illā mubṭilūna
versets.trad: We have set forth for people in this Qurʾān every kind of example; yet if you bring them a sign, those who disbelieve will surely say: “You are nothing but impostors!”
versets.commentaire: The Qurʾān has multiplied examples for people, but the disbeliever turns the sign into an accusation. In Qushayrī's perspective, this obstinacy reveals a sealed heart: the clearest proof avails nothing to one who bears within himself a deafness to the Reality; they call impostors the very ones who bring them the truth.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.def: ḍaraba mathalan: to give a parable.
mubṭil: one who promotes the vain.

versets.ar: كَذَلِكَ يَطْبَعُ اللَّهُ عَلَى قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: kadhālika yaṭbaʿu llāhu ʿalā qulūbi lladhīna lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Thus does God seal the hearts of those who do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, toward the end of the sūra, sees in this seal the term of their obstinacy: He has announced their persistence and their engulfment in their error, and that such is their portion of the apportionment to the end of their lives. The sealing of the heart is no caprice, but the bolt set upon an ignorance chosen and maintained.

versets.ar: فَاصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَلَا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ṣbir inna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun wa-lā yastakhiffannaka lladhīna lā yūqinūna
versets.trad: So be patient. Surely the promise of God is true. And let not those who lack certainty unsettle you!
versets.commentaire: The sūra is sealed on the command of patience addressed to the Prophet. Qushayrī illumines it thus: after announcing their obstinacy and their engulfment in error, God closes the sūra by commanding His Messenger to endure the bearing of their harms and injuries. The promise of God is true (ḥaqq): let no heart without certainty come to shake the firmness of the believer.

mots.racine: و ع د / ح ق ق
mots.sens: the promise of God is true

mots.def: istakhaffa: to make waver, to make light of.
